zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, latterly Wester Hoprew - 1st Lord Hay of Yester於1450年出生在英國蘇格蘭皮布萊爾時，他的父親Sir David Hay of Lochorwart and Yester57歲，母親Elizabeth Mary Douglas of Angus, latterly of Forbes, then Lochorwort and Yester52歲。他於1462年7月7日之前與Mariot Lindsay of Lindsay of the Byres結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1508年10月23日在英國蘇格蘭易亞斯特 洛迪安也斯特吉福德享年58歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾也斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, latterly Wester Hoprew - 1st Lord Hay of Yester于1450年出生在英国苏格兰皮布萊爾时，他的父亲Sir David Hay of Lochorwart and Yester57岁，母亲Elizabeth Mary Douglas of Angus, latterly of Forbes, then Lochorwort and Yester52岁。他于1462年7月7日之前与Mariot Lindsay of Lindsay of the Byres结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1508年10月23日在英国苏格兰易亞斯特 洛迪安也斯特吉福德享年58岁，埋葬在苏格兰哈丁頓史爾也斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Hay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English (northern; of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in Normandy called La Haie or La Haye (Old French haie ‘hedge, enclosure’, ‘forest for hunting deer and other animals’, a borrowing of the ancient Germanic word haga). Robert de Haia or de la Haye is known to have come from La Haye-du-Puits in Manche; he was the founder of Boxgrove Priory in Sussex (1123), and holder of the Honor of Halnaker (Sussex) and (by marriage) the barony of Kolswein (Lincolnshire). The Norman name was also taken to Ireland, where it has since flourished in the county of Wexford as Hay and Hayes . Elsewhere in Ireland the name usually has a native Irish origin, see below.

English: topographic name from Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg) or ‘forest fenced off for hunting’ (Old French haie); or else a habitational name from a place so called, such as Hay (in Herefordshire and Westmorland) or Hey in Scammonden (Yorkshire). It was no doubt sometimes synonymous with Hayward .
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah).

variants.name: High
meanings: English:
nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hay 3.
topographic name for someone who lived ‘(on the) height or hill’, from Middle English heigh ‘height, hilltop’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hight .

variants.name: Hai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Abdul, Syed, Arifa, Atta, Faiz, Hamid, Iftekhar, Iskandar, Mohamed, Mohammad, Mohannad.  Vietnamese Mui, Pham, Tran, Vo, Bui, Cao, Chau, Dinh, Duong, Nguyen, Sau, Vo Van.  Jewish Ilan, Ronen, Shlomo.  Chinese Chan, Chiming, Lung, Wai Fun, Yin.
Muslim (mainly the Indian subcontinent): from a personal name based on Arabic ḥayy ‘alive’. Al-Ḥayy ‘the Living’ is an attribute of Allah.
Jewish: variant of   Hay  .

variants.name: Dehay
meanings: French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone who lived by an enclosure, from the masculine form of Old French haye ‘hedge’; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word (see Hay 9).
Altered form (also De Hay) of Dutch De Haij or De Haaij, which is probably of French origin (see above).

variants.name: Haig
meanings: Scottish and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of various places in northern France named with Old Norse hagi ‘enclosure’, a word with cognates in most ancient Germanic languages. Compare Hay . The Haigs of Bemersyde in Berwickshire derive their name from the district of La Hague in Manche.
Scottish and Irish (Cavan): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Thaoig and Mac Thaidhg (see McCaig ).

variants.name: Hayman
meanings: English: topographic name probably from Middle English hai(e), hei(e) ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg), denoting a dweller by an enclosure (see Hay 2 ), or else a nickname for a ‘keeper of the enclosure’ (compare Hayward ).
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, high(e) (Old English hēah); see also Hay 3.
English: occupational name for a seller of hay, from Middle English hai, hei (Old English hēg, hīg).

variants.name: Hayes
meanings: Irish (Cork): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hAodha ‘descendant of Aodh’, a personal name meaning ‘fire’. Compare McCoy . In some cases especially in County Wexford, the surname is of English origin (see below), having been taken to Ireland by the Normans.
English: variant of Hay , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

English: topographic name from the plural form of Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (see Hay ), sometimes used as a collective noun for a farm, especially in Devon, where it is a frequent minor placename. Compare Hain .

variants.name: Haycock
meanings: English: from a pet form of the Middle English personal name Hai + coc; see Hay 4 and compare Cocke .

variants.name: Haylett
meanings: English (Norfolk): from a pet form (double diminutive) of the personal name Hai (see Hay 4), from Hai + -el + -ot.

variants.name: Hey
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hay  .
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Heij, a topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, Dutch hei, heide.
German: metonymic occupational name for a grower or mower of grass, from Middle High German höu ‘grass, hay’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, latterly Wester Hoprew - 1st Lord Hay of Yester was born in 1450, in Peeblesshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Sir David Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, was 57 and his mother, Elizabeth Mary Douglas of Angus, latterly of Forbes, then Lochorwort and Yester, was 52. He married Mariot Lindsay of Lindsay of the Byres before 7 July 1462. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 23 October 1508, in Gifford, Yester, East Lothian, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 58, and was buried in Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland.

fr.Gifford, Yester, East Lothian, Scotland,: Gifford, Yester, East Lothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, latterly Wester Hoprew - 1st Lord Hay of Yester est né en 1450, à Valle de Twede, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir David Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, avait 57 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Mary Douglas of Angus, latterly of Forbes, then Lochorwort and Yester, avait 52 ans. Il a épousé Mariot Lindsay of Lindsay of the Byres avant 7 juillet 1462. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 23 octobre 1508, à Gifford, Yester, East Lothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterré à Yester, Haddingtonshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, latterly Wester Hoprew - 1st Lord Hay of Yester 1450 in Peeblesshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir David Hay of Lochorwart and Yester 57 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Mary Douglas of Angus, latterly of Forbes, then Lochorwort and Yester 52. Er hat vor 7. Juli 1462 Mariot Lindsay of Lindsay of the Byres geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 23. Oktober 1508 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Gifford, Yester, East Lothian, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, latterly Wester Hoprew - 1st Lord Hay of Yester è nato il nel 1450 nel Peeblesshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir David Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, aveva 57 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Mary Douglas of Angus, latterly of Forbes, then Lochorwort and Yester, aveva 52 anni. Lui sposato Mariot Lindsay of Lindsay of the Byres prima del 7 luglio 1462. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 23 ottobre 1508, nel Gifford, Yester, East Lothian, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 58 anni, e fu sepolto a Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, latterly Wester Hoprew - 1st Lord Hay of Yesterが1450年にイギリススコットランドペエブレシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir David Hay of Lochorwart and Yesterは57歳，母Elizabeth Mary Douglas of Angus, latterly of Forbes, then Lochorwort and Yesterは52歳であった。1462年7月7日以前にMariot Lindsay of Lindsay of the Byresと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1508年10月23日にイギリススコットランドエアスト ロスアンイエスターギフォードで58歳で死去し，スコットランドハッドイントンシュアイアイエスターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, latterly Wester Hoprew - 1st Lord Hay of Yester(이)가 1450년 영국 스코틀랜드 피블레실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir David Hay of Lochorwart and Yester(은)는 57세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Mary Douglas of Angus, latterly of Forbes, then Lochorwort and Yester(은)는 52세였다. 그는 1462년 7월 7일 이전 Mariot Lindsay of Lindsay of the Byres(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1508년 10월 23일 영국 스코틀랜드 이스트 로스이언 예스터 지퍼드에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 하딩토실르 예스터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, latterly Wester Hoprew - 1st Lord Hay of Yester nasceu em 1450, em Peeblesshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir David Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, tinha 57 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Mary Douglas of Angus, latterly of Forbes, then Lochorwort and Yester, tinha 52 anos. Ele casou-se com Mariot Lindsay of Lindsay of the Byres antes 7 de julho de 1462. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 23 de outubro de 1508, em Gifford, Yester, East Lothian, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 58 anos, e foi sepultado em Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland.

ru.Gifford, East Lothian, Scotland: гиффорд, иестир, эаст лотян, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.Gifford, Yester, East Lothian, Scotland,: гиффорд, иестир, эаст лотян, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, latterly Wester Hoprew - 1st Lord Hay of Yester родился в 1450 г. в местности пиблессхир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir David Hay of Lochorwart and Yester было 57 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Mary Douglas of Angus, latterly of Forbes, then Lochorwort and Yester, 52. Он женился на женщине по имени Mariot Lindsay of Lindsay of the Byres перед 7 июля 1462 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 23 октября 1508 г. в местности гиффорд, иестир, эаст лотян, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 58 лет и был похоронен в местности иестир, хаддингтоншир, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, latterly Wester Hoprew - 1st Lord Hay of Yester nació en 1450, en Peeblesshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir David Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, tenía 57 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Mary Douglas of Angus, latterly of Forbes, then Lochorwort and Yester, tenía 52 años. Se casó con Mariot Lindsay of Lindsay of the Byres antes de 7 de julio de 1462. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 23 de octubre de 1508, en Gifford, Yester, East Lothian, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 58 años, y fue sepultado en Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland.
